speaker: MENENIUS
text: No, I’ll not go. You hear what he hath said

text: But what o’ that? Go you that banished him;

text: The way into his mercy. Nay, if he coyed

text: To hear Cominius speak, I’ll keep at home.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: He would not seem to know me.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Do you hear?

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Yet one time he did call me by my name.

text: I urged our old acquaintance, and the drops

speaker: COMINIUS
text: That we have bled together. “Coriolanus”

speaker: COMINIUS
text: He would not answer to, forbade all names.

speaker: COMINIUS
text: He was a kind of nothing, titleless,

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Till he had forged himself a name o’ th’ fire

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Of burning Rome.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Why, so; you have made good work!

speaker: MENENIUS
text: A pair of tribunes that have wracked Rome

text: To make coals cheap! A noble memory!

text: I minded him how royal ’twas to pardon

speaker: COMINIUS
text: It was a bare petition of a state

speaker: COMINIUS
text: To one whom they had punished.

text: Very well.

text: Could he say less?

speaker: COMINIUS

speaker: COMINIUS
text: For ’s private friends. His answer to me was

speaker: COMINIUS
text: He could not stay to pick them in a pile

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Of noisome musty chaff. He said ’twas folly

speaker: COMINIUS
text: For one poor grain or two to leave unburnt

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And still to nose th’ offense.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: For one poor grain or two!

speaker: MENENIUS
text: I am one of those! His mother, wife, his child,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: And this brave fellow too, we are the grains;

speaker: MENENIUS
text: You are the musty chaff, and you are smelt

speaker: MENENIUS
text: Above the moon. We must be burnt for you.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Nay, pray, be patient. If you refuse your aid

text: In this so-never-needed help, yet do not

text: Upbraid ’s with our distress. But sure, if you

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Would be your country’s pleader, your good tongue,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: More than the instant army we can make,

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Might stop our countryman.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: No, I’ll not meddle.

speaker: SICINIUS
text: Pray you, go to him.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: What should I do?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Only make trial what your love can do

text: For Rome, towards Martius.

text: Well, and say that Martius

text: What then? But as a discontented friend,

text: Grief-shot with his unkindness? Say ’t be so?

text: Must have that thanks from Rome after the measure

text: As you intended well.

text: I think he’ll hear me. Yet to bite his lip

text: And hum at good Cominius much unhearts me.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: He was not taken well; he had not dined.

text: Than in our priestlike fasts. Therefore I’ll watch him

text: Till he be dieted to my request,

text: And then I’ll set upon him.

text: You know the very road into his kindness

text: Good faith, I’ll prove him,

text: I tell you, he does sit in gold, his eye

text: Red as ’twould burn Rome; and his injury

text: The jailor to his pity. I kneeled before him;

text: For mercy to his country. Therefore let’s hence
